analysis.problem: TypeScript compilation error in DynamicContentCarousel.tsx: The PostSelectorSortable component's onChange callback has the signature `(ids: number[] | number) => void`, but the current handler at line 206-208 passes the value directly to setAttributes({ selectedPosts: value }). The selectedPosts attribute is typed as `number[]`, so assigning `number | number[]` to it causes a type error.
analysis.rootCause: The PostSelectorSortable component (from @taktdev/components) is designed to handle both single-select (returns a number) and multi-select (returns a number[]) modes. Its onChange type is `(ids: number[] | number) => void`. When used without a `limit` prop (multi-select mode), it typically returns a number[], but the type system requires handling both cases.

referencePattern.note: FeaturedPost uses limit={1} (single-select mode) and handles the union by checking typeof value === 'number'. For DynamicContentCarousel, the inverse pattern is needed: ensure the result is always an array.

changes.description: Add type guard to PostSelectorSortable onChange handler to safely coerce the `number | number[]` return value into `number[]` before assigning to selectedPosts attribute.
changes.currentCode:
<PostSelectorSortable
	value={ attributes.selectedPosts }
	onChange={ ( value ) =>
		setAttributes( { selectedPosts: value } )
	}
	postTypes={ [ attributes.postsType ] }
/>
changes.proposedCode:
<PostSelectorSortable
	value={ attributes.selectedPosts }
	onChange={ ( value ) =>
		setAttributes( {
			selectedPosts: Array.isArray( value )
				? value
				: value
					? [ value ]
					: [],
		} )
	}
	postTypes={ [ attributes.postsType ] }
/>
changes.rationale: Array.isArray(value) handles the number[] case (pass through). The ternary `value ? [value] : []` handles the number case: wrap a valid post ID in an array, or return empty array for 0/falsy values. This matches the pattern from FeaturedPost.tsx but adapted for multi-select (array) output.

decision.decision: Use Array.isArray() type guard with falsy check for single number values
decision.evidence: FeaturedPost.tsx lines 146-151 uses typeof check for single-select. Adapted pattern for multi-select: Array.isArray(value) ? value : (value ? [value] : [])

otherOnChangeHandlersAudit.description: Audit of all other onChange handlers in DynamicContentCarousel.tsx for similar type issues

handlers.code: onChange={ ( value ) => setAttributes( { postsType: value as string } ) }
handlers.note: Uses `as string` cast. ToggleGroupControl onChange returns `string | number | undefined`. The cast is acceptable here since all options are strings.

handlers.code: onChange={ ( value ) => setAttributes( { postsSource: value as string } ) }
handlers.note: Same pattern as above. Acceptable cast.
TaxonomyMultiSelect's onChange type should be checked separately but is not part of this ticket.
RangeControl onChange returns `number | undefined`. The postsLimit attribute is typed as `number`. Minor potential undefined issue but not in scope.
ToggleControl onChange returns boolean, matches attribute type.
Custom onChange with Partial<DynamicContentCarouselAttributes> type. Properly typed.

otherOnChangeHandlersAudit.summary: Only one onChange handler (PostSelectorSortable at lines 204-211) has the type mismatch issue. All other handlers are correctly typed or use acceptable casts.

testimonialsCarousel.note: Exact same pattern: PostSelectorSortable onChange passes value directly to setAttributes({ selectedPosts: value }). Will be fixed in a separate pipeline as stated in the change request.

testPlan.description: This is a type-safety-only fix. No visual or functional changes occur.
steps: Verify TypeScript compilation succeeds after the change (no type errors in DynamicContentCarousel.tsx)
Verify the block editor still allows selecting and deselecting posts in manual mode
Verify selectedPosts attribute is correctly saved as an array after selection
testPlan.reason: Pure TypeScript type guard addition with no runtime behavior change. The PostSelectorSortable already returns arrays in multi-select mode; this fix just satisfies the type checker.
